edges.description: PIGL loss impairs GPI anchor formation and reduces cell-surface GPI anchor markers.
Developmental GPI-anchor deficiency is associated with the cardinal ocular coloboma phenotype.
Developmental GPI-anchor deficiency is associated with congenital heart defects.
GPI-anchor deficiency in ectodermal tissues is associated with ichthyosiform dermatosis.
Neurodevelopmental effects of GPI-anchor deficiency are associated with intellectual disability.
Ear involvement in CHIME includes conductive hearing loss.
Neurologic involvement in CHIME includes seizures.
Developmental involvement in CHIME includes typical facial features.
Multisystem developmental involvement can include renal collecting-system anomalies.
Growth involvement in CHIME can include infantile macrosomia.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the glycosylphosphatidylinositol gene PIGL cause CHIME syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CHIME syndrome is characterized by colobomas, heart defects, ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation (intellectual disability), and ear anomalies

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the glycosylphosphatidylinositol gene PIGL cause CHIME syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CHIME syndrome is characterized by colobomas, heart defects, ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation (intellectual disability), and ear anomalies, including conductive hearing loss

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the glycosylphosphatidylinositol gene PIGL cause CHIME syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CHIME syndrome is characterized by colobomas, heart defects, ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation (intellectual disability), and ear anomalies

evidence.reference_title: Neuroectodermal (CHIME) syndrome: an additional case with long term follow up of all reported cases.
evidence.snippet: The main features include ocular colobomas, congenital heart disease, early onset migratory ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation, conductive hearing loss, seizures, and typical facial features.

nodes.description: Reduced levels of GPI-anchored proteins on cell surface due to impaired biosynthesis, affecting normal cellular functions and signaling pathways. This leads to the multisystem phenotype characteristic of CHIME syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the glycosylphosphatidylinositol gene PIGL cause CHIME syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, cell lines derived from these cases had significantly reduced levels of the two GPI anchor markers, CD59 and a GPI-binding toxin, aerolysin (FLAER), confirming the pathogenicity of the mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the glycosylphosphatidylinositol gene PIGL cause CHIME syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CHIME syndrome is characterized by colobomas, heart defects, ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation (intellectual disability), and ear anomalies

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the glycosylphosphatidylinositol gene PIGL cause CHIME syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CHIME syndrome is characterized by colobomas, heart defects, ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation (intellectual disability), and ear anomalies

evidence.reference_title: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia in a child with the CHIME neuroectodermal dysplasia syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Her major problems included a migratory ichthyosiform dermatosis, multiple skin infections and infestations, bilateral retinal coloboma, developmental delay, seizures, infantile macrosomia, facial anomalies, a duplicated renal collecting system, and conductive hearing loss.

nodes.description: Mutations in PIGL gene disrupt glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI) anchor biosynthesis. PIGL encodes a de-N-acetylase required for GPI anchor formation, essential for anchoring various cell surface proteins including receptors, enzymes, and adhesion molecules.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the glycosylphosphatidylinositol gene PIGL cause CHIME syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CHIME syndrome is characterized by colobomas, heart defects, ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation (intellectual disability), and ear anomalies, including conductive hearing loss. Whole-exome sequencing on five previously reported cases identified PIGL, the de-N-acetylase required for glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI) anchor formation, as a strong candidate.

evidence.reference_title: Neuroectodermal (CHIME) syndrome: an additional case with long term follow up of all reported cases.
evidence.snippet: The main features include ocular colobomas, congenital heart disease, early onset migratory ichthyosiform dermatosis, mental retardation, conductive hearing loss, seizures, and typical facial features.

evidence.reference_title: Neuroectodermal (CHIME) syndrome: an additional case with long term follow up of all reported cases.
evidence.snippet: Long term follow up information on this patient, as well as the previously described cases, provides information regarding the outcome for these patients, which includes general good health, severe mental retardation, seizures that worsen after puberty, conductive hearing loss, and chronic migratory ichthyosiform skin rash without scarring.
